title: imagebuilder: split Executor and per-stage execution
body:
When building images using multiple stages, we were using a shallow copy of the `Executor`, depending on some fields to be shared between all instances, while replacing others fields with per-stage values.

This patch makes that easier for me to reason about by introducing a `StageExecutor` which holds the stage-specific information, and which can hold a reference to the main `Executor`, which now coordinates stages using `StageExecutors` rather than `Executors`.

comments.body: build issue appears to be a timeout flake.
passes baseline and the other tests I threw at it.  LGTM, but would like a head nod from @vrothberg in the morning.
I restarted the two failed tests, so we can merge the PR once everything's green.

:pushpin: Commit aa58e3c has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan aa58e3c68f06be89c9aec3d3fbaffc9ebc569fcc -->

Removed logic to reset the stage's volume cache each time we commit a layer in a stage - the cached contents of parts of the working container's rootfs don't need to be regenerated just because we committed a layer and created a new container from the image, since we know that that portion of the new container;s directory tree is supposed to have the same contents as the old one.
